Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF AMANDA CHENG'S Motion To Compel Compliance, And For Monetary Sanctions

Motion to compel DENIED. I conclude that there was a reasonable dispute over whether the extension to March 1 applied to these discovery requests. Based on that reasonable dispute, it would be excessive to order that objections are waived. It is inappropriate to raise substantive disputes about those responses in a Reply. Rather, the parties should meet and confer of the responses served on March 1 and file another motion if necessary.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by e-mail to the Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Aly Hadibhai And Oncap Management Partners, Lps Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions

MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFF AMANDA CHENG Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Aly Hadibhai And Oncap Management Partners, Lps Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Production And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions

Motion to compel DENIED. I conclude that there was a reasonable dispute over whether the extension to March 1 applied to these discovery requests. Based on that reasonable dispute, it would be excessive to order that objections are waived. It is inappropriate to raise substantive disputes about those responses in a Reply. The parties should meet and confer of the responses served on March 1 and file another motion if necessary.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 by e-mail to the Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman at sborrowman@gmail.com.

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Aug-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF AMANDA CHENG MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE

Plaintiff Amanda Cheng's motion to admit counsel Brennwyn Romano pro hac vice, which is unopposed, is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The application does not include the information required under California Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
